text.tspan: GATEKEEPER: a dedicated service stands between the public internet
and the trusted internal services. Only its responsibility:
validate, authenticate, authorize, sanitize, and forward.
u2022 small, well-audited, hardened u2192 if compromised, attacker still has no DB/secrets
u2022 business logic NOT in the security layer (easier to reason about)
u2022 security updates go to ONE service, not every business service
u2022 DEFENSE IN DEPTH: even if gatekeeper is bypassed,
business services should still check auth (belt + braces)
API Gateway (a gatekeeper that ALSO does routing/aggregation)
WAF (Web Application Firewall u2014 a more network-layer gatekeeper)
Only job: be the security perimeter.
u2022 VALIDATE input (JSON schema, size limits, content type)
u2022 LOG every request for audit
u2022 NEVER speak to the database directly
u2022 MINIMAL code footprint u2192 smaller attack surface
